primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable recognised in consolidated financial statements can be designated as a hedged item under IFRS hedge accounting.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what items may be designated as hedged items in hedge accounting, including the specific rule and exception for intragroup items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain when exchange differences on monetary items, including intragroup monetary items in consolidation, are recognised and therefore clarify the underlying exposure relevant to hedge accounting.
These paragraphs clarify the distinct hedge accounting model for a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation.

peripheral_authority.reason: This interpretation addresses distributions of non-cash assets by an entity to its owners, not hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivables.
These paragraphs concern other IFRS 9 topics such as embedded derivatives, measurement and presentation, or hedge mechanics not needed to identify the peer approaches here.
These paragraphs concern disclosure and share-based payment matters rather than hedge accounting eligibility.
These paragraphs address broader foreign currency translation matters not necessary for identifying the top-level hedge accounting approaches.
These paragraphs relate to statement of cash flows classification and disclosures, not hedge accounting eligibility.
This interpretation concerns tax consequences of changes in tax status, which is unrelated to the issue.
These paragraphs concern provisions and executory contracts, not hedge accounting for intragroup foreign exchange exposures.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk on a recognised monetary item
Hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure forming part of a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised receivable is a recognised item that can fall within the general IFRS hedge accounting models for designated hedged items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for qualifying exposures.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS requirements treat a net investment hedge as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign exchange exposures linked to a foreign operation.
